toleranceRationale: Ceilings were seeded from a dev-machine (Apple M4 Pro) measured p95, and per-metric headroom deliberately VARIES: millisecond-scale metrics (tree-render, guard-call) carry ~5-7x; sub-millisecond metrics (tree-validate, store, apps-api, gen-scripted) get absolute floors of 1-8ms, which works out to ~9-250x relative headroom because relative multipliers are meaningless at microsecond scales where a single scheduler blip dwarfs the signal. The gate catches order-of-magnitude regressions (accidental O(n^2), a dropped index, a renderer that stopped memoizing), not jitter. These are provisional dev-machine-derived values: once .github/workflows/perf.yml has produced a few runs on main, recalibrate each ceiling against real CI-runner p95 samples (procedure in bench/README.md). To legitimately raise a budget, open a PR editing this file with the new measured p95 and a one-line justification (what changed and why the higher cost is expected).
